focus: Micro-Phonetics Refinement — General Vowel Reduction & Overall Fluency Integration
focus_ar: تحسين الصوتيات الدقيقة — تخفيض حروف العلة العامة ودمج الطلاقة الشاملة
arabic_challenge: This week completes the Table 10/§5.6 micro-phonetics refinement cycle (flap T Week 1, dark L Week 2, schwa Week 3) with a general vowel-reduction and full-fluency integration focus — testing whether ALL your accumulated skills (from L0 through L3) work together seamlessly in professional communication contexts (this week's vocabulary theme), the most demanding real-world register you'll regularly need.

isolation.instructions: Professional vocabulary (circle back, touch base, bandwidth) often has multiple reducible vowels in casual business speech — practice applying full reduction naturally.
isolation.instructions_ar: مفردات مهنية زي circle back و touch base غالبًا فيها حروف علة قابلة للتخفيض في الكلام التجاري العادي — تدرب على تطبيق التخفيض الكامل بشكل طبيعي.

sentence_practice: Let's circle back and touch base about our bandwidth this week.
We need to streamline this deliverable by Friday.
I'll touch base once we circle back on the bandwidth issue.
daily_drills.record_this: Let's circle back and touch base about our bandwidth — we need to streamline this deliverable by Friday.
daily_drills.self_check: Did professional vocabulary reduce as naturally as everyday vocabulary you've practiced since earlier levels?

target_sounds: full-fluency integration: all connected-speech skills in a business role-play

isolation.instructions: Role-play a realistic business meeting scenario, deliberately checking for flap T, dark L, schwa, linking, and reduction all working together.
isolation.instructions_ar: مثّل سيناريو اجتماع عمل واقعي، وتأكد بشكل متعمد من الفلاب T والـ L الغامقة والشوا والربط والتخفيض كلهم شغالين مع بعض.
practice: Deliver a mock status update: 'I wanted to touch base and circle back on the deliverable timeline.'

sentence_practice: I wanted to touch base and circle back on the deliverable timeline.
Let's align on the action items before we escalate this further.
We should get buy-in from stakeholders before moving forward.
daily_drills.record_this: I wanted to touch base and circle back on the deliverable timeline — let's align on the action items before we escalate this further.
daily_drills.self_check: Did all your accumulated connected-speech skills work together naturally in this professional context?

target_sounds: vowel reduction and fluency under professional pressure (negotiation simulation)

isolation.instructions: Test fluency under simulated pressure — a negotiation or a slightly tense professional exchange, since real professional communication often happens under some pressure.
isolation.instructions_ar: اختبر الطلاقة تحت ضغط محاكى — مفاوضة أو تبادل مهني فيه شوية توتر، لأن التواصل المهني الحقيقي غالبًا بيحصل تحت ضغط.
practice: Simulate: 'I understand your position, but I need to push back on this deadline.'

sentence_practice: I understand your position, but I need to push back on this deadline.
Let's escalate this if we can't align on the deliverable.
I hear you, but the bandwidth just isn't there this week.
daily_drills.record_this: I understand your position, but I need to push back on this deadline — let's escalate this if we can't align.
daily_drills.self_check: Under the simulated pressure of a negotiation tone, did your fluency and connected speech hold up?

target_sounds: comprehensive fluency check: cold-reading a professional document aloud

isolation.instructions: Read a real, unfamiliar professional document (an email, article, or report) aloud cold, checking for overall fluency and natural connected speech throughout.
isolation.instructions_ar: اقرأ مستند مهني حقيقي وغير مألوف (إيميل أو مقال أو تقرير) بصوت عالي وبدون تحضير، وتأكد من الطلاقة الشاملة والكلام المتصل الطبيعي.
practice: Find and read aloud a real professional email or short report you have access to.

pair: confident cold-reading with full connected speech

sentence_practice: (No fixed sentences — uses your own chosen professional text.)
daily_drills.record_this: (Read a real, unfamiliar professional document aloud, cold, with no preparation.)
daily_drills.self_check: Across this real, unrehearsed text, which specific skills (from any level) felt weakest? Note them for personal review.

target_sounds: full spontaneous professional communication (mock interview or pitch)

isolation.instructions: Deliver a spontaneous 60-90 second mock job interview answer or elevator pitch, integrating every accumulated fluency skill under realistic professional stakes.
isolation.instructions_ar: قدم إجابة عفوية لمقابلة عمل تجريبية أو عرض مصعد لمدة ٦٠-٩٠ ثانية، ودمج كل مهارات الطلاقة المتراكمة تحت ضغط مهني واقعي.
practice: Answer, unscripted: 'Tell me about yourself and why you'd be a good fit for this role.'

daily_drills.record_this: (Free speech: answer 'Tell me about yourself' as if in a real job interview, for 60-90 seconds.)
daily_drills.self_check: Under real interview-style pressure, did your full accumulated skill set (flap T, dark L, schwa, linking, reduction, intonation) come through naturally?

daily_drills.target_sounds: Review: professional vocabulary reduction, role-play integration, negotiation pressure, cold-reading, mock interview
challenge_sentences: I wanted to touch base and circle back — I understand your position, but I need to push back on this deadline given our bandwidth.
Let's align on the action items, streamline the deliverable, and escalate only if we can't get buy-in.
Tell me about yourself — well, I'd say I'm someone who likes to get the ball rolling and always tries to move the needle.
daily_drills.record_this: I wanted to touch base and circle back — I understand your position, but given our bandwidth, I need to push back on this deadline.
daily_drills.self_check: This is a comprehensive professional-context review. Score your overall fluency integration across this passage.

daily_drills.target_sounds: Self-Assessment: Micro-Phonetics Refinement Completion Checkpoint (All Skills Integrated)

test_yourself.instructions: Record a 90-second spontaneous mock professional scenario of your choice (interview, negotiation, or presentation), integrating every skill refined across L3 Weeks 1-4.
test_yourself.passage: (Free speech: 90 seconds, a realistic professional scenario of your choice, fully spontaneous.)

scoring_guide.90_100: (Gold/Platinum tier) All refined skills work together seamlessly under real professional pressure and spontaneity — this is genuinely native-level fluency.
scoring_guide.70_89: (Silver tier) Strong overall integration; minor lapses under the highest-pressure moments.
scoring_guide.50_69: Individual skills solid but integration under pressure still requires conscious effort.
scoring_guide.below_50: Return to the specific weak skill's earlier-level material (L1/L2) for targeted review before continuing.

for_arabic_speakers: This week completes the 4-week Micro-Phonetics Refinement cycle (Table 10/§5.6) — you have now specifically stress-tested flap T, dark L, schwa, and general fluency each under unfamiliar vocabulary, emotional/comedic pressure, and full spontaneity.
Professional communication is often cited as the register where non-native speakers feel the most self-conscious — that's exactly why this week's integration test uses it as the capstone context.

From here, L3 Weeks 5-8 shift focus from phonetic refinement to broader native-like fluency patterns: rhetoric, cultural nuance, advanced connected speech theory, and finally full mastery integration.
supplementary_notes.recording_requirement: Record Day 5 and Day 7 — this Day 7 recording is a genuine Table 11 mastery-tier benchmark, worth comparing against your very first L0 recordings for a full-journey perspective.
